summary: Soundboard recording of decent quality captures a high‑energy New Year’s Eve 1977 show with smoking Scarlet→Fire→Truckin'→Wharf Rat and standout Jack Straw, NFA and Friend of the Devil performances.
review: The SBD capture is generally clear and well‑balanced – a few reviewers note a rough start but most describe the sound as good enough to enjoy the entire night. Musically the show is praised for its nonstop rock drive, with Jerry “on fire” and a blistering Scarlet/Fire/Truckin'/Wharf Rat sequence that many fans cite as a highlight; other favorites include Jack Straw, Not‑Fade‑Away and Friend of the Devil. While a handful of comments are critical (especially one negative take on Truckin'), the overwhelming tone is positive.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (≈70 %) give 4‑5 stars and highlight strong musical moments; recording quality is described as good/clear by most, with only occasional minor complaints.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp and balanced soundboard feed; a few reviewers mention a rough opening or modest drop‑outs but overall the mix is clear enough to hear individual instruments and vocals.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans note a relentless, rocking flow with no space‑y jams – a tight New Year’s Eve set that keeps the energy high from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly called “on fire”, loud and delivering scorching guitar work throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's piano solo on Friend of the Devil is singled out as a sweet, melodic highlight.
band_member_comments.Drums: The two‑drummer era (Billy & Mickey) gets mixed remarks – praised for Billy’s swing feel and solid rhythm, while some feel the dual setup adds too much shuffle.
